title: Wallet names are globally unique instead of scoped per user
reasoning: The wallet model has a required user ForeignKey, but the unique constraint is only on name. That means once any user creates a wallet named, for example, 'Cash', every other user is blocked from using the same wallet name, which violates the apparent per-user ownership model used by related_name='wallets' and the category/budget uniqueness rules that include user.
reproduction: Create two users, create Wallet(name='Cash', user=user1), then try to create Wallet(name='Cash', user=user2). The second insert fails with a uniqueness violation even though the wallets belong to different users.
recommendation: Change the wallet uniqueness constraint to include user, for example unique_together={('name', 'user')}, or add an equivalent UniqueConstraint scoped by user.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No tests were included for this feature, so there is no evidence that multi-user wallet naming behavior is covered.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a migration/model test or Django TestCase that creates two different users and verifies each can create a wallet with the same name, while duplicate wallet names for the same user are still rejected.
minimumFixScope: Add a new migration altering the Wallet uniqueness constraint from ('name',) to ('name', 'user').
